annotations.label: Box–Muller transform

anchor.snippet: is a random number sampling method for generating pairs of independent

annotations.note: No Box–Muller transform construction exists in Mathlib; grepping `Mathlib` for `Box.?Muller` returns no files.

annotations.label: Basic form setup

anchor.snippet: Suppose U 1 and U 2 are independent samples chosen from the uniform distribution on the unit interval

annotations.note: Mathlib has uniform distributions and independence of random variables, but does not formalize this specific Box–Muller setup.

annotations.label: Z0 and Z1 are independent standard normal

anchor.snippet: Then Z 0 and Z 1 are independent random variables with a standard normal distribution

annotations.note: The Box–Muller output theorem is absent from Mathlib, although `gaussianReal` (in `Mathlib.Probability.Distributions.Gaussian.Real`) defines the standard normal.

annotations.label: Polar coordinate derivation

anchor.snippet: the random variables for R 2 and Θ (shown above) in the corresponding polar coordinates are also independent

annotations.note: No statement about polar-coordinate transformation of a 2D Gaussian giving independent radial-squared and angular components is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: R^2 chi-squared / exponential

anchor.snippet: Because R 2 is the square of the norm of the standard bivariate normal variable

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `exponentialPDF`/`expMeasure` but has no chi-squared distribution and no theorem identifying the squared norm of a 2D standard Gaussian with an exponential law.

annotations.label: Polar form construction

anchor.snippet: Given u and v , independent and uniformly distributed in the closed interval

annotations.note: The Marsaglia polar-form rejection construction is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: s uniformly distributed on (0,1)

anchor.snippet: the values of s will be uniformly distributed in the open interval

annotations.note: No formalization of the polar-form claim that s = u² + v² conditioned on the unit disk is uniform on (0,1).

annotations.label: Angle θ uniform and independent of s

anchor.snippet: Equally so, the angle θ divided by

annotations.note: The independence of the angle and radius for the polar-form rejection sample is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polar form produces standard normal deviates

anchor.snippet: Just as the basic form produces two standard normal deviates, so does this alternate calculation

annotations.note: The polar-form algorithm and its correctness theorem are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polar method rejection rate

anchor.snippet: It discards 1 − π /4 ≈ 21.46% of the total input uniformly distributed random number pairs generated

annotations.note: The numeric rejection-rate fact 1 − π/4 for the polar form is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cost of basic form

anchor.snippet: The basic form requires two multiplications, 1/2 logarithm , 1/2 square root , and one trigonometric function for each normal variate

annotations.note: Operation-count complexity claims about the basic Box–Muller form are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cost of polar form

anchor.snippet: The polar form requires 3/2 multiplications, 1/2 logarithm, 1/2 square root, and 1/2 division for each normal variate

annotations.note: Operation-count complexity claims about the polar form are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tail truncation bound

anchor.snippet: This means that the algorithm will not produce random variables more than 6.660 standard deviations from the mean

annotations.note: Finite-precision tail-truncation bounds for Box–Muller are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.note: Mathlib contains no executable C++ code; this implementation example is out of scope.
